Table Support

Complete documentation for Excel tables (ListObjects) in Aspose.Cells FOSS for .NET

← Back to Index

Table Overview

The ListObject class represents an Excel table (structured reference / ListObject) with built-in filtering, sorting, and styling capabilities.

Table Features

  • Built-in Filters: Auto-filter dropdown buttons for table headers
  • Built-in Styles: 60+ professional table styles with banding options
  • Total Row: Support for totals row with built-in calculations
  • Table Styles: Style first and last columns, row and column stripes
  • Structured References: Refer to table data using table name in formulas

Usage Examples

Creating a Simple Table

using Aspose.Cells_FOSS;

var workbook = new Workbook();
var worksheet = workbook.Worksheets[0];

// Add headers
worksheet.Cells["A1"].PutValue("Name");
worksheet.Cells["B1"].PutValue("Age");
worksheet.Cells["C1"].PutValue("City");

// Add data rows
for (int i = 0; i < 5; i++)
{
    worksheet.Cells[i + 2, 0].PutValue($"Person {i + 1}");
    worksheet.Cells[i + 2, 1].PutValue(new Random().Next(20, 60));
    worksheet.Cells[i + 2, 2].PutValue($"City {new Random().Next(10, 20)}");
}

// Create a table
int tableIndex = worksheet.ListObjects.Add(0, 0, 3, 1, true);

// Configure table
var table = worksheet.ListObjects[tableIndex];
table.DisplayName = "People Data";
table.ShowHeaderRow = true;
table.ShowTotals = false;

Console.WriteLine($"Table '{table.DisplayName}' created");

Adding Multiple Columns

// Access a specific column
var table = worksheet.ListObjects[0];

// Add data to column
for (int i = 0; i < 10; i++)
{
    var column = table.ListColumns[i];
    if (column != null)
    {
        Console.WriteLine($"Column {i}: Not defined");
        continue;
    }
    column.Name = $"Column {i + 1}";

    // Set formula for totals calculation
    column.TotalsCalculation = TotalsCalculation.Sum;
    column.NumberFormat = "#,##0";
}

Console.WriteLine($"Added {table.ListColumns.Count} columns");

Using Table Styles

using Aspose.Cells_FOSS;

var workbook = new Workbook();
var worksheet = workbook.Worksheets[0];

// Add data
for (int i = 0; i < 10; i++)
{
    worksheet.Cells[i, 0].PutValue($"Item {i + 1}");
    worksheet.Cells[i, 1].PutValue(new Random().Next(100, 1000));
}

// Create a table with style
int tableIndex = worksheet.ListObjects.Add(0, 0, 3, 1, true);

// Apply a built-in style
var table = worksheet.ListObjects[tableIndex];
table.TableStyleType = TableStyleType.TableStyleMedium9;
table.ShowTableStyleFirstColumn = true;
table.ShowTableStyleLastColumn = true;
table.ShowTableStyleRowStripes = true;
table.ShowTableStyleColumnStripes = false;

Console.WriteLine($"Applied style: {table.TableStyleType}");

Table Properties

The ListObject class provides the following properties:

Basic Properties
  • DisplayName: User-visible table name
  • Comment: Optional table description
  • StartRow/Column: Top-left cell position
  • EndRow/Column: Bottom-right cell position
  • ShowHeaderRow: Show/hide header row
  • ShowTotals: Show/hide totals row
Advanced Properties
  • TableStyleType: Built-in table style
  • TableStyleName: Raw table style name
  • ShowTableStyleFirstColumn: Style first column
  • ShowTableStyleLastColumn: Style last column
  • ShowTableStyleRowStripes: Row banding
  • ShowTableStyleColumnStripes: Column banding
Methods
  • ListColumns: Get all table columns
  • Resize: Resize table to new range
  • ShowAutoFilter: Enable auto filter
  • RemoveAutoFilter: Disable auto filter
  • ConvertToRange: Convert table to data range

ListObjectCollection

The ListObjectCollection class manages all tables in a worksheet:

  • Count: Number of tables
  • this[int]: Access table by index
  • Add() - Add a new table
  • Parameters: table range and headers flag

ListColumnCollection

The ListColumnCollection class manages columns within a table:

  • Properties: Name, NumberFormat, Formula, TotalsCalculation
  • Methods: Add new column, get column by name

TableStyleType Enumeration

Built-in table styles:

Default Styles
  • None: No special formatting
  • TableStyleMedium9: Medium blue style
  • TableStyleLight1: Light blue style
  • TableStyleLight9: Light gray style
  • TableStyleMedium7: Medium green style
  • TableStyleDark1: Medium dark gray style
  • TableStyleDark11: Dark gray style
  • TableStyleLight6: Light green style
  • TableStyleLight11: Light gray style
  • TableStyleMedium2: Medium green style
  • TableStyleLight3: Light red style
  • TableStyleMedium3: Medium red style
  • TableStyleDark3: Dark red style
Color Styles
  • TableStyleLight2: Light blue style
  • TableStyleDark4: Dark blue style
  • TableStyleDark6: Dark purple style
  • TableStyleLight8: Light green style
  • TableStyleDark8: Dark green style
Professional Styles
  • TableStyleLight11: Light gray style
  • TableStyleDark11: Dark gray style
  • TableStyleLight13: Light blue style
  • TableStyleDark13: Dark blue style
Custom Styles
  • TableStyleType.Custom: Custom table style
  • TableStyleName: Custom style name
  • Preserves: Setting Custom preserves current TableStyleName

TotalsCalculation Enumeration

Totals calculation methods for table columns:

  • None: No calculation
  • Sum: Sum of values
  • Average: Average of values
  • Count: Count of non-empty cells
  • CountNums: Count of numeric cells
  • Min: Minimum value
  • Max: Maximum value
  • StdDev: Standard deviation
  • Var: Variance

Important Notes

  • Modern Chart Types: Not supported for programmatic table creation
  • Formula References: Tables support structured references in formulas
  • Resize Behavior: Resizing a table updates the underlying data range
  • Multiple Tables: Worksheets can contain multiple tables
  • Table Persistence: Table data is persisted in the worksheet model
